findings.message: Routes module imports from item_service (a newly introduced duplicate data-access layer) that replicates functionality already present in repository.py (fetch_all_items vs list_items, add_item vs create_item). This creates architectural coupling to a redundant module and violates DRY principles.
findings.suggestion: Consolidate the data-access layer: use the existing repository.py module consistently throughout the application. Remove item_service.py and update imports to use repository.list_items and repository.create_item instead.

findings.message: Routes handler calls fetch_all_items from the duplicate item_service module instead of list_items from the canonical repository module. This perpetuates the duplicate data-access layer problem.
findings.suggestion: Replace fetch_all_items(session) with list_items(session) and update the import on line 8 to use repository module.

findings.message: Routes module calls add_item from the duplicate item_service module instead of create_item from the canonical repository module. Both modules now offer nearly identical data-access functions, creating maintenance burden and architectural confusion.
findings.suggestion: Replace add_item(session, payload.name) with repository.create_item(session, payload.name) and remove the session.commit() and session.refresh() calls in the handler—let the data-access layer manage transaction semantics (as repository.create_item already does).
